summary: For irregular float32 matmul shapes, directly feeding non-aligned ND tensors into the generic Matmul high-level API can produce localized correctness mismatches on tail regions even when compilation succeeds. A reliable fix is to keep the AscendC kernel as a regular aligned matmul and, in python_bind, zero-pad A/B to 128-aligned M/K/N sizes before EXEC_NPU_CMD, then slice the padded output back to the original shape.

Do not try to fix irregular ND matmul correctness first by forcing usedCoreNum launches plus manual SetTail logic on top of the generic IterateAll path; that can amplify output corruption because the block scheduling assumptions no longer match the simple kernel wrapper. Revert to the proven single-core launch wrapper and handle irregularity through zero-padding/cropping in python_bind instead.
